methods.method_name: Botanical Identification of Fossil Pollen Grains
methods.description: This method involves the botanical identification of fossil pollen grains through detailed morphological analyses and comparisons with modern taxa, allowing identifications at various taxonomic levels.

steps.description: Perform detailed morphological analyses of fossil pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze morphology of fossil pollen grains

steps.description: Compare morphological data with modern taxa to identify pollen at genus, species, or family level.
steps.searchable_summary: compare pollen morphology with modern taxa

methods.description: This method statistically compares past pollen assemblages with a database of modern pollen records to estimate climatic parameters, providing a climatic range and a 'most likely value' for the climate.

steps.description: Access a database of more than 8,000 modern pollen records from various latitudes and longitudes in the Northern Hemisphere.
Statistically compare each past pollen assemblage with the modern pollen records to transpose relative abundances into climatic values.
steps.searchable_summary: compare past pollen with modern records for climate estimation

steps.description: Calculate the most probable climate for a set of taxa, providing a climatic range and a 'most likely value' as a weighted mean.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate probable climate from pollen data

methods.description: This method involves processing sediments, identifying pollen, and constructing synthetic pollen diagrams to illustrate vegetation changes.

steps.description: Process sediments using gentle techniques and mount residue between cover-slip and microscope slide in glycerol.
steps.searchable_summary: process sediments and prepare slides for pollen analysis

steps.description: Identify pollen using modern pollen atlases and, if necessary, scanning electron microscope (SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen using atlases and SEM

steps.description: Count at least 100-150 pollen grains and construct synthetic pollen diagrams to illustrate vegetation changes.
steps.searchable_summary: construct pollen diagrams from grain counts
